name: Hook Development
tagline: Reference guide for building Claude Code plugin hooks (PreToolUse, Stop, SessionStart, etc.) with prompt- and command-based patterns.
verdict: Thorough, safe reference with concrete JSON/bash examples and a security-practices section (path traversal, quoting, sensitive-file denial). Weak spots: non-kebab-case name, no stated exclusions, SKILL.md runs ~713 lines past the size budget, and a dense 600+ char description.

highlights.summary: No 'NOT for' / exclusion guidance anywhere despite a broad, multi-trigger description.
Full inline examples for every hook event type live in SKILL.md even though references/ files exist for detailed patterns.
name: 'Hook Development' is not kebab-case (contains a space and capital letters).
